item.title: Scam Alerts Deceiving Users to Download Harmful Android Applications
Why Your "Unmovable" Assets Need Zero Trust Access with SMA 1000
PDF-Borne Living-off-the-Land Attacks with RMM Abuse
Stop Comparing Features. Start Demanding Outcomes
item.description: Buying more security tools doesn’t guarantee protection. See why outcomes depend on integration, management and continuous delivery.

item.title: Budibase Cloud View Filter eval() Injection Remote Code Execution (CVE-2026-27702)
item.description: CVE-2026-27702 is a critical (CVSS 9.9) remote code execution vulnerability in Budibase versions prior to 3.30.4. Attackers can inject malicious JavaScript through view filter values, which are passed unsanitized to eval() via the in-memory view subsystem in cloud mode. SonicWall Capture Labs provides threat analysis, exploitation details, IPS signature protection (IPS: 22007), and remediation guidance.

item.title: SecureFirst Partner Program Updates for 2026
item.description: SonicWall updates its SecureFirst Partner Program with new specializations, role-based accreditations and expanded opportunities for partners.

item.title: Microsoft Security Bulletin Coverage for March 2026
SonicWall Earns 5-Star Rating in 2026 CRN Partner Guide
item.description: SonicWall’s SecureFirst Partner Program earns a 5-Star rating in the 2026 CRN Partner Program Guide, recognizing exceptional partner enablement.

item.title: The Why and What of FIPS 140-3 Validation in Modern Cybersecurity
Deno Runtime Exploited: The Emerging Threat You Can’t Ignore
An In-Depth Look at Scarface Stealer
Michael Crean Discusses Iran Cyber Threats on Fox News
item.description: SonicWall SVP Michael Crean appeared on Fox News to discuss Iran-linked cyber activity and the Stryker attack. Watch the full interview.

item.title: The SonicSentry SOC in Action in the Morning
item.description: An alert may seem small, but tackling it quickly could be the difference between a good day and a very bad one.

item.title: FIPS 140-3 Validation: The Difference Between Proven and Assumed
Juniper JunOS OS Evolved Pre-authenticated Remote Code Execution (CVE-2026-21902)
item.description: The SonicWall Capture Labs threat research team became aware of a severe unauthenticated Incorrect Permission Assignment for Critical Resource vulnerability in Juniper Networks Junos OS Evolved (PTX Series), assessed their impact, and developed mitigation measures. Juniper PTX Series routers are high-performance core and peering routers built for high throughput, low latency, and scale. They are commonly used by internet service providers, telecommunication services, and cloud network applications.

item.title: Partner Power News - March Momentum: Driving Growth & Secure Access for MSPs | SonicWall
item.description: Boost MSP success with modern cybersecurity, secure‑by‑default strategies, SonicWall firewall leadership, and new SecureFirst Partner Program updates driving growth.

item.title: Three Decades for a 3-Line Fix: The Critical telnetd Bug Hiding in Plain Sight (CVE-2026-32746)
item.description: The SonicWall Capture Labs threat research team became aware of an out-of-bounds write vulnerability in the Telnet server shipped with GNU Inetutils, assessed its impact and developed mitigation measures. Telnetd hardly needs an introduction. It is one of the oldest and most widely distributed network utilities on Linux systems. The issue, tracked as CVE-2026-32746, affects all telnetd versions up to 2.7. This vulnerability, classified as a classic buffer overflow (CWE-120), allows an unauthenticated remote attacker to crash the server and potentially execute arbitrary commands. It carries a critical CVSS score of 9.8. Contrary to popular belief that Telnet is dead, it remains hidden within the vital infrastructure that runs the world. Hence, users are strongly encouraged to apply the vendor-provided patch or restrict/disable the telnet access without delay.

item.title: From Threat Report to Protect Report: What It Means for SMBs and Partners
item.description: Discover how the 2026 Cyber Protect Report shifts focus from threats to outcomes, and what it means for SMBs and partners.

item.title: Future-Proof Your SMB: Moving from Legacy Firewalls to Active Protection
item.description: Discover why SMBs are choosing active protection over reactive "break-fix" security. Save time with seamless Gen 6 migration tools and the "3 & Free" program.

item.title: GPT Academic Pickle Deserialization Remote Code Execution (CVE-2026-0763)
item.description: SonicWall Capture Labs analyzes CVE-2026-0763, a critical CVSS 9.8 unauthenticated remote code execution vulnerability in GPT Academic v3.91 and earlier. The flaw chains a TAR symlink path traversal in the upload handler with unsafe pickle deserialization in the Social Helper plugin, allowing attackers to execute arbitrary commands without authentication. IPS signature 22081 provides protection.

item.title: Manage Firewalls, APs & Switches in One Platform
item.description: Manage firewalls, access points, and switches in one platform. Simplify operations, improve visibility, and scale faster with SonicWall Unified Management.

item.title: Unpacking the Nursultan Client PyInstaller Telegram Malware
How SonicWall's Knowledge Base Powers Faster, Smarter Customer Support
item.description: Discover how SonicWall's KCS-certified knowledge base delivers 5,000+ articles to help partners and customers resolve issues quickly and confidently.

item.title: n8n Expression Sandbox Bypass RCE - CVE-2026-1470
item.description: Critical CVSS 9.9 RCE vulnerability in n8n AI workflow automation via expression sandbox bypass. SonicWall IPS signatures IPS:21799 and IPS:22086 available.

item.title: SonicWall Named Leader in 2026 GigaOm Firewall Radar
item.description: SonicWall is named a Leader and Fast Mover in the 2026 GigaOm Radar for Enterprise Firewalls, highlighting innovation and proven threat protection.

item.title: ACRStealer The Silent Golang Threat Behind Credential and Wallet Theft
Microsoft Security Bulletin Coverage for April 2026
User Clicks Lead to Breaches – How Zero Trust Stops Execution-Based Attacks
item.description: Phishing links and malicious files remain a leading cause of ransomware. Learn how Zero Trust access helps prevent user-initiated attacks from becoming breaches.

item.title: Credential Theft Is Inevitable – How Zero Trust Access Prevents Breaches
item.description: Credential compromise is one of the most common causes of data breaches. Learn how stolen credentials are used in attacks and how Zero Trust access helps stop them.

item.title: VPN Vulnerabilities Lead to Breaches – How to Eliminate Risk
item.description: Unpatched VPN vulnerabilities continue to drive ransomware attacks and business disruption. Learn how modern secure access eliminates exposed infrastructure and reduces breach risk.

item.title: Firewall Security Assessment Reporting: How SonicWall NSM Strengthens Your Network Security Posture
item.description: Discover how firewall security assessment reporting with SonicWall NSM 3.5 surfaces threat trends, network health gaps, and productivity risks — in one report.

item.title: Finding Vulnerabilities at Scale: How SonicWall Built an AI-Assisted Security Pipeline for Large C Codebases
item.description: Learn how SonicWall built an AI‑assisted, multi‑stage security pipeline to find real vulnerabilities at scale in large C codebases—where architecture, not models, does the heavy lifting.

item.title: Ghost CMS Content API Blind SQL Injection - CVE-2026-26980
item.description: Critical CVSS 9.4 unauthenticated blind SQL injection in Ghost CMS Content API (v3.24.0-6.19.0) enables full database read. SonicWall IPS signatures available.

item.title: What Are PoPs and Why Do They Matter for Zero Trust Security?
item.description: Points of Presences (PoPs) are the backbone of cloud-delivered security. Learn what PoPs are, why they matter, and how CSE's new PoP Management gives your team full visibility and control.

item.title: Take Action on the 7 Deadly Cyber Sins With the 2026 SonicWall Protect Report
item.description: Our threat data shows organizations make the same cyber mistakes over and over. Here are the steps you can take to overcome them.

item.title: Automated Cybersecurity Response: Why Detection Isn’t Enough
item.description: Cyber threats move fast. Learn why detection alone isn’t enough and how automated security response helps stop attacks before they spread.

item.title: Langflow Public Flow Build Endpoint RCE - CVE-2026-33017
item.description: Critical CVSS 9.8 unauthenticated RCE in Langflow AI via build_public_tmp endpoint passes user input to exec() without sandboxing. SonicWall IPS signatures available.

item.title: All-Port TLS Inspection: SonicWall RFDPI and DPI-SSL Explained
item.description: Over 90% of traffic is encrypted. Threats run on port 4444, 8443, or wherever they choose. See how SonicWall RFDPI and DPI-SSL inspect every port, every session.

item.title: Superior Management, Elevated Security: A Partner’s Early Experience with SonicWall Network Security Manager 3.0
item.description: Leaf IT shares its experience with SonicWall Network Security Manager 3.0

item.title: Security as a Story: Using Reporting to Prove Your Value as an MSP
item.description: Learn how reporting can help you tell your MSP story effectively so your customers experience your true value.

item.title: What Is a Virtual Firewall? Cloud & VM Security Explained
item.description: Learn how virtual firewalls secure east-west traffic across VMware, AWS, Azure, and Kubernetes—where traditional edge firewalls fall short.

item.title: Why Multi-Engine Cloud AV Is the Only Reliable Defense Against Modern Malware | SonicWall
item.description: Single antivirus engines miss 2-15% of novel malware. Learn how SonicWall’s multi-engine cloud AV architecture (Gateway AV, Capture ATP, Capture Client, and MDR) delivers layered protection across gateway, sandbox, and endpoint.

item.title: A Long Time Ago in a Galaxy Far, Far Away…Cybersecurity Was Already Hard
SonicWall Gen 6 End of Life: The Credential Visibility Gap Putting Your Network at Risk
item.description: 1 in 10 SonicWall devices running Credential Auditor found bad credentials — and Gen 6 firewalls can’t run it. Learn why upgrading to Gen 7 is a critical security decision, not just a hardware refresh.

item.title: Partner Power News - May 2026: Protection. Transition. Scale. | SonicWall
item.description: Turn insight into action with Partner Power News May 2026. Cyber Protect insights, product transitions, EPSS on Gen 7 TZ, SaaS milestones, and new enablement resources to scale impact and pipeline.

item.title: Virtual Firewall vs. Physical Firewall: How to Choose the Right Protection for Cloud, On-Premises, and Hybrid Networks
item.description: Virtual firewalls deliver cloud agility. Physical firewalls deliver throughput and control. Learn the differences, use cases, and how to deploy both together.

item.title: How SonicWall's Hybrid Mesh Firewall Aligns with Gartner's CSMA Framework
item.description: Discover how SonicWall Unified Management enables a Hybrid Mesh Firewall architecture that maps directly to Gartner's Cybersecurity Mesh Architecture principles for distributed, cloud-ready security.

item.title: The Death of the Firewall — Why It Isn't Going Anywhere | SonicWall
item.description: The firewall has been declared dead for decades. So why is the market still growing? SonicWall CPO Chandrodaya Prasad explains why it is not going anywhere.

item.title: Mesop AI Sandbox Unauthenticated RCE - CVE-2026-33057
item.description: Critical CVSS 9.8 unauthenticated RCE in Google Mesop AI Sandbox /exec-py endpoint allows arbitrary Python execution. SonicWall IPS signature available.

item.title: The Ransomware Chimera That Does Everything
item.description: This week, the SonicWall Capture Labs Threat Research team examined a .NET executable that combines ransomware, botnet, information stealer, and worm capabilities. The sample is particularly notable for explicitly referencing eight different ransomware families throughout its codebase.
